steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A line with slope $m$ and $y$-intercept $b$ can be written as
$$y=mx+b$$

steps.title: Substitute the line facts
steps.content:
The slope is $-3$. The point $(0,80)$ is the $y$-intercept, so $b=80$.
$$y=-3x+80$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Use the two points $(0,80)$ and $(1,77)$ to confirm the line has slope $-3$ and intercept $80$. The answer is A.
